insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 192, 'Trekking on the island: 3 must-try routes to fully appreciate the beauty of Cat Ba.', '

The activity of island trekking in Cat Ba offers a diverse experience with 3 main routes: the National Park - Ao Ech - Viet Hai forest crossing route, the Ngu Lam Peak - Kim Giao climbing route, and the Cat Co coastal route. These are the most ideal itineraries for visitors to explore the forest ecosystem on limestone mountains, train their health and rediscover balance amid pristine nature.

 

Welcome to Cat Ba – the "Green Pearl" of the Gulf of Tonkin. If you think traveling to Cat Ba is only about sea bathing and eating seafood, then you've missed 70% of the true beauty of this place. The most pristine and majestic soul of the island lies deep within the old forests and atop the steep limestone peaks.

 

Why does Cat Ba trekking attract nature-loving travelers?

 

Before going into the details of the routes, we need to understand why this activity has become a "hot" trend in recent years. More than just walking, Cat Ba trekking is a genuine healing journey.

 

Cat Ba is a convergence of diverse ecosystems: tropical rainforest on a limestone island, mangrove forests, coral reefs and a system of caves. When taking part in island trekking, you not only train your physical strength but also get to breathe fresh air with a pollution index of almost zero.

 

 

Details of the 3 most beautiful island trekking routes in Cat Ba

 

Below are 3 island trekking itineraries designed to suit various fitness levels, from beginners to those with "restless feet".

 

1. The National Park - Ao Ech - Viet Hai Village route (The classic Cat Ba trekking route)

 

Highlights of the journey:



    • Warm-up: Starting from the National Park gate, you'll climb over short slopes to advance deep into the forest core.

 

    • Ao Ech (Frog Pond): After about 2 hours of climbing, Ao Ech appears like an ink-wash painting. This is the only freshwater lake located halfway up the limestone mountain, with crystal-clear water you can see to the bottom, surrounded by dense vegetation. It is a wonderful rest stop to check in and listen to the echoing sounds of frogs and insects.

 

    • Cat's-ear rock slope: The stretch from Ao Ech to Viet Hai is a real challenge with sharp cat's-ear rocks (jagged limestone). You need to watch every step.

 

    • Viet Hai Village: The end of the forest journey is the lush green Viet Hai valley. An ancient fishing village completely isolated from the outside world, unusually peaceful.



Note: This route requires good fitness and endurance. After reaching Viet Hai, you can choose to take a boat or canoe back to Beo Wharf instead of walking back.

 

2. The Ngu Lam Peak - Kim Giao Forest route (A short trek with panoramic views)

 

If you don't have too much time but still want to experience island trekking and admire the panorama of Cat Ba from above, then this is the number 1 choice.

 

Route characteristics:



    • Duration: About 2 - 3 hours round trip.

 

    • Kim Giao Forest: You'll walk through the Kim Giao forest – a precious timber species once used to make chopsticks for kings of old to test for poison. The straight trunks and interwoven canopies create a cool green dome that shields the sunlight.

 

    • Ngu Lam Peak: From the Kim Giao forest, you climb a further rocky slope to reach Ngu Lam Peak. Here, your view expands 360 degrees. The undulating limestone ranges stretch endlessly like dragon's teeth plunging into the blue sea. The feeling of the strong wind and the majestic scenery will dispel all fatigue.



This Cat Ba trekking route is suitable for families with children (aged 10 and up) or groups of friends who want to take "Instagram-worthy" photos.

 

3. The Cat Co coastal route (Gentle coastal trekking)

 

Unlike the two forest-crossing routes above, this route brings the salty flavor of the sea. It is the path connecting the Cat Co 1, 2 and 3 beaches.

 

Relaxing experience:



    • The path is built along the mountainside, with a steep cliff on one side and the vast blue sea on the other.

 

    • You can stroll leisurely while watching the sunset fall over Lan Ha Bay.

 

    • This is the gentlest island trekking route, requiring no skills or fitness, perfect for an afternoon walk.



This route proves that Cat Ba trekking is not only about sweat and steep slopes, but also about romantic and relaxing moments.

 

In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

 

 

When is the ideal time to take part in Cat Ba trekking?

 

For a complete trip, choosing the right time is extremely important. The weather directly affects safety and scenery when you go island trekking.



    • Autumn and early winter (September - November): This is the "golden time". The weather is cool, with little rain and gentle sunshine, extremely suitable for mountain climbing. The forest is also lush at this time after the summer rainy season.

 

    • Spring (January - March): The weather is chilly, sometimes with fog creating a magical scene. However, the path can be a bit slippery due to high humidity, so you need to pay attention to your footwear.

 

    • Summer (May - August): The peak season for beach tourism but quite hot for trekking. If you go during this season, you should set off very early (around 5-6 a.m.) to avoid the harsh sun and the risk of heat stroke.



Note: Avoid going Cat Ba trekking on stormy, rainy days because the forest trails are very slippery, the cat's-ear rocks become dangerous, and there's a risk of landslides.

 

 

A guide to packing for your island trekking trip

 

Thorough preparation will determine 50% of the trip's success. Below is a list of "indispensable" items when you take part in Cat Ba trekking:



    • Specialized trekking shoes: Absolutely do not wear flip-flops, high heels or leather shoes. Choose shoes with good grip (deep tread) because the limestone terrain is very sharp and slippery.

 

    • Clothing: Cool, sweat-wicking clothes. You should wear long pants and long sleeves to avoid insects and scratches from plants.

 

    • Drinking water and snacks: Bring enough 1.5 - 2 liters of water per person. Snacks like dry rations, chocolate and nuts to replenish energy along the way.

 

    • Insect repellent: The Cat Ba forest has quite a few mosquitoes and leeches, especially after rainy days. Bring insect repellent spray (Soffell or essential oils).

 

    • Trekking poles (if available): Help reduce pressure on the knees when going downhill.

 

    • First aid kit: Bandages, antiseptic, basic stomachache medicine.



 

 

 

Safety and cultural notes when going Cat Ba trekking

 

To protect yourself and preserve the beauty of island trekking, keep the following principles in mind:



    • Don't litter: "Take nothing but photos, leave nothing but footprints." Carry all plastic bottles and food packaging out of the forest.

 

    • Go in groups: You shouldn't go alone, especially on the long Ao Ech - Viet Hai route.

 

    • Follow the signs: Don't enter prohibited areas or unexploited trails to avoid getting lost.

 

    • Respect wildlife: If you encounter langurs or other wild animals, observe from a distance, don't tease them or feed them strange food.

 

    • Hire a guide: If you don't have experience with Cat Ba trekking, hiring a local guide is very necessary. They not only lead the way but also tell you interesting stories about the island's history and biology.



 

 

 

Frequently asked questions about Cat Ba trekking tourism

 

Below are quick answers to common questions to help you feel more confident before setting out.

 

1. Can children go Cat Ba trekking?

 

Yes, but it depends on the route. The Kim Giao - Ngu Lam route or the Cat Co coastal path are suitable for children aged 6-10 and up in good health. The Viet Hai route is long and difficult, and is not recommended for young children.

 

2. How much is the entrance ticket for Cat Ba National Park?

 

Currently, the entrance fee is around 80,000 VND/adult and 40,000 VND/child. This price may change depending on the time.

 

3. Do I need to book a trekking tour or can I go independently?

 

You can absolutely go independently if you have experience and travel in a group. However, booking a tour through reputable providers will give you a full package of shuttle transport, meals and higher safety assurance, especially on the long routes.

 

4. How long does Cat Ba trekking take?

 

It depends on the route you choose. The shortest route is about 1 hour, the longest forest-crossing route can take 4-6 hours. You should dedicate a full day or a long morning to this activity.

 

5. Is island trekking dangerous?

 

The limestone mountain terrain has certain risks of slipping. However, if you follow the safety rules and stay on the planned route, this is a safe and enjoyable activity.

 

 

Island trekking: 3 routes you must try to see all the beauty of Cat Ba is not just an article title, but an invitation for you to step out of your comfort zone and touch nature. No matter which route you choose, the feeling of conquering a mountain peak and gazing at the vast sea and sky will be an unforgettable memory.

 

Cat Ba awaits you with the mysteries of the old forest and the openness of the sea. Grab your backpack and go to find yourself small yet full of life amid majestic nature.

 

If you're looking for a professional trekking tour operator that combines soul-healing therapies amid Cat Ba's nature, contact us right away. We design journeys not just to travel, but to feel and regenerate energy.

 

 

LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited

 

Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !

 

Address: 102 My Dinh, Cau Giay, Hanoi

 

Phone: 0986662282

 

Email: support@virenaretreat.com Website: virenaretreat.com

    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Frequently asked questions about Cat Ba trekking tourism</span></h2>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Below are quick answers to common questions to help you feel more confident before setting out.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">1. Can children go Cat Ba trekking?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Yes, but it depends on the route. The Kim Giao - Ngu Lam route or the Cat Co coastal path are suitable for children aged 6-10 and up in good health. The Viet Hai route is long and difficult, and is not recommended for young children.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">2. How much is the entrance ticket for Cat Ba National Park?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Currently, the entrance fee is around 80,000 VND/adult and 40,000 VND/child. This price may change depending on the time.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">3. Do I need to book a trekking tour or can I go independently?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">You can absolutely go independently if you have experience and travel in a group. However, booking a tour through reputable providers will give you a full package of shuttle transport, meals and higher safety assurance, especially on the long routes.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">4. How long does Cat Ba trekking take?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">It depends on the route you choose. The shortest route is about 1 hour, the longest forest-crossing route can take 4-6 hours. You should dedicate a full day or a long morning to this activity.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">5. Is island trekking dangerous?</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">The limestone mountain terrain has certain risks of slipping. However, if you follow the safety rules and stay on the planned route, this is a safe and enjoyable activity.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/trekking-tren-dao-3-cung-duong-phai-thu-de-thay-het-ve-dep-cua-cat-ba-3.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="675" /></span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Island trekking: 3 routes you must try to see all the beauty of Cat Ba</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> is not just an article title, but an invitation for you to step out of your comfort zone and touch nature. No matter which route you choose, the feeling of conquering a mountain peak and gazing at the vast sea and sky will be an unforgettable memory.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Cat Ba awaits you with the mysteries of the old forest and the openness of the sea. Grab your backpack and go to find yourself small yet full of life amid majestic nature.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">If you're looking for a professional trekking tour operator that combines soul-healing therapies amid Cat Ba's nature, contact us right away. We design journeys not just to travel, but to feel and regenerate energy.</span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"><img src="/uploads/0000/1/2025/12/30/trekking-tren-dao-3-cung-duong-phai-thu-de-thay-het-ve-dep-cua-cat-ba-4.jpg" alt="" width="900" height="500" /></span></p>\r\n
    <p> </p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.9871999999999999; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited </span></p>\r\n
